task: No-Gold RIGOURATE + Scalar Baseline Pipeline Part 1
nli_scalar_blocked_reason: no HCM/NLI features available in repo
scalar_distinguishes_mild_strong_evidenc: rule_scalar ROC-AUC=0.5054 if available; llm_scalar ROC-AUC=0.5874 if available; threshold 0.60; result=False
scalar_detects_strong_action_evidence: rule_scalar recall=0.7838 F1=0.3791 if available; llm_scalar recall=0.6200 F1=0.3974 if available; threshold recall>=0.30 and F1>=0.20; result=True
main_remaining_risk: Scalar proxy baseline confirms that scalar scores cannot replace hierarchical relation typing (mild_vs_strong ROC-AUC < 0.60, strong_action ROC-AUC < 0.55). However, the proxy is built on silver labels and a 200-sample LLM subset; gold adjudication may shift conclusions. The NLI scalar is blocked, leaving a gap in the proxy comparison. Main paper risk: if reviewers argue the proxy is too weak to be a fair baseline, the inability-to-replace-relation-typing claim weakens.
recommended_next_action: Proceed to Part 2 (public benchmark comparison if any public dataset becomes available) OR proceed to gold pilot (50-pair two-layer relation+realism annotation) to validate the mild_vs_strong boundary that scalar cannot resolve. Do NOT claim proxy as official RIGOURATE. Do NOT claim scalar results as gold.
